title: potentilla × collina subsp. wibeliana
intro: Potentilla × collina subsp. wibeliana, commonly known as the hybrid cinquefoil, is a hybrid plant species in the family Rosaceae, resulting from the cross between Potentilla collina and Potentilla fruticosa. The plant contains isoflavones, particularly potentilones, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Potentilla × collina subsp. wibeliana include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce menopausal symptoms, and exhibit antioxidant activity. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and dried extracts, which can be used to leverage its medicinal properties.
